tool_input.content:
# Fork divergence — RaggioS/jcode

This is a personal fork of [1jehuang/jcode](https://github.com/1jehuang/jcode) used as the sole
local-model coding harness driving Ollama `gemma4:12b` on macOS. It tracks upstream `master` and carries
a small set of changes on the `feat/local-gemma4-macos` branch.

## Carried patches (vs upstream)

1. **macOS image paste on non-English locales** — `crates/jcode-tui/src/tui/app/helpers.rs`,
   `crates/jcode-tui/src/tui/app/input.rs`.
   - `clipboard_image()`: AppleScript-ObjC reader → **JXA** (`osascript -l JavaScript`). The AppleScript
     dialect failed to parse on non-English system locales (Italian raised `-2741 "found plural class name"`),
     so image paste silently returned nothing. JXA syntax is locale-independent.
   - `handle_paste()`: when a bracketed paste delivers **empty text** (how macOS / the VS Code integrated
     terminal report Cmd+V for an image-only clipboard), read the clipboard image and attach it. Safe only
     for empty text — non-empty pastes keep the upstream Wayland multi-MIME guard.
   - Result: Cmd+V (and Ctrl+V) attach screenshots in the VS Code terminal on macOS-Italian. Good
     upstream-PR candidate.

## Runtime configuration (NOT in this repo — machine-local, templated in `pocket-llm/jcode/`)

- **Thinking off** for gemma4: Ollama `/v1` honors top-level `reasoning_effort`. Set it via a named provider
  in `~/.jcode/config.toml` (`[providers.ollama-local] extra_body = { reasoning_effort = "none" }`) or the
  `JCODE_OPENAI_EXTRA_BODY='{"reasoning_effort":"none"}'` env in the launcher.
- **Telemetry off**: `DO_NOT_TRACK=1`.
- **Italian persona**: jcode loads `~/AGENTS.md` (global) + project `AGENTS.md` into the system prompt
  (`crates/jcode-base/src/prompt.rs`). The lean Italian persona lives there.

## Keeping in sync with upstream

```bash
git fetch upstream
git rebase upstream/master            # on feat/local-gemma4-macos
cargo build --release --bin jcode
scripts/install_release.sh
```

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>" && echo "committed" && git log --oneline -3

cd "/Users/raimondospatola/Documents/AI Projects/jcode" && export PATH="/opt/homebrew/bin:$PATH" && echo "build start $(date +%T)" && cargo build --release --bin jcode 2>&1 | tail -8; echo "EXIT=${PIPESTATUS[0]} end $(date +%T)"

cd "/Users/raimondospatola/Documents/AI Projects/pocket-llm" && git checkout -b feat/jcode-launcher 2>&1 | head -1 && echo "=== pyproject hermes refs ===" && grep -niE "hermes" pyproject.toml && echo "=== scripts dir ===" && ls scripts/ && echo "=== download_model refs hermes? ===" && grep -liE "hermes" scripts/* 2>/dev/null; echo "=== HERMES.md size ===" && wc -l vscode-extension/HERMES.md

cd "/Users/raimondospatola/Documents/AI Projects/jcode" && gh pr create --repo RaggioS/jcode --base master --head feat/local-gemma4-macos --title "macOS image paste fix (locale-independent) + fork config" --body "## What
Fork customization branch for running jcode as the local-gemma4 coding harness on macOS.

## Changes
- **fix(tui): macOS image paste on non-English locales.** Two clipboard fixes:
  - \`clipboard_image()\`: the embedded AppleScript-ObjC reader failed to parse on non-English system locales (Italian raised \`-2741 'found plural class name'\`), so image paste silently returned nothing. Replaced with **JXA** (\`osascript -l JavaScript\`), whose syntax is locale-independent.
  - \`handle_paste()\`: when a bracketed paste delivers empty text (how macOS / the VS Code integrated terminal report Cmd+V for an image-only clipboard), read the clipboard image and attach it. Safe only for empty text — non-empty pastes keep the upstream Wayland multi-MIME guard.
- **docs: PATCHES.md** tracking fork divergence and the rebase-from-upstream workflow.

## Impact
Cmd+V (and Ctrl+V) attach screenshots in the VS Code terminal on macOS-Italian, which stock upstream drops. The clipboard fix is a clean upstream-PR candidate.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)" --assignee RaggioS 2>&1 | tail -3

pocket-llm's launcher now drives **jcode** (RaggioS fork, Rust harness), NOT Hermes, for the local gemma4 lane.
Decision after a full eval (see [[jcode-eval]]): jcode ties Hermes on gemma4 speed (model-bound) but adds native
multi-agent/swarm + a lighter footprint; the user adopts jcode for local gemma4 and keeps Hermes ONLY for the
ccr-free-router Gemini lane ([[ccr-free-router-repo]]).

- **`vscode-extension/extension.js`**: opens the jcode TUI (`jcode --provider ollama --model gemma4:12b`) in a
  parallel editor column; status button now "$(rocket) jcode". Ollama lifecycle UNCHANGED (RAM-aware
  `OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH`, `KEEP_ALIVE=30m`, flash-attn + q8_0 KV, model pre-warm). Hermes thinking-patch reapply
  REMOVED. Env sets `DO_NOT_TRACK=1` + `JCODE_OPENAI_EXTRA_BODY={"reasoning_effort":"none"}` (thinking off).
- **`jcode/`** (replaces `hermes/`): `config.toml` (→`~/.jcode/config.toml`: provider ollama-local, gemma4:12b,
  `extra_body.reasoning_effort="none"`, ctx 16384), `AGENTS.md` (→`~/AGENTS.md`: lean Italian persona — jcode loads
  `~/AGENTS.md` + project `AGENTS.md`, `jcode-base/src/prompt.rs`), `env.sample`. Removed `hermes/`,
  `scripts/reapply-thinking-patch.sh`, `vscode-extension/HERMES.md` (→ new `JCODE.md`).
- **jcode binary**: built/installed from fork [[jcode-eval]] / [[jcode-fork]] (`~/.local/bin/jcode`). Thinking-off,
  telemetry-off, macOS image paste fixed (2 patches). Same Ollama backend, same gemma4:12b, same ~8s warm.
- Everything below this block (Hermes config, thinking-patch, sampling) is HISTORICAL — applies to the Hermes lane
  that moved to ccr-free-router, not to pocket-llm anymore.

tool_input.content:
---
name: jcode-fork
description: RaggioS/jcode fork — our customized jcode (Rust coding harness) for the local gemma4 lane. Build/install, config, patches, rebase-from-upstream.
metadata:
  type: reference
---

# jcode (RaggioS fork) — operational reference

Our personal fork of [1jehuang/jcode](https://github.com/1jehuang/jcode) = the **sole local-gemma4 coding
harness** (replaced Hermes for that lane 2026-06-25; eval → [[jcode-eval]]). MIT, Rust. Upstream is a
single-author project; we own the fork so our patches are durable + rebase-able.

## Repos / locations
- Fork: `https://github.com/RaggioS/jcode` (remote `origin`); upstream `https://github.com/1jehuang/jcode` (remote `upstream`).
- Working clone: **`$WS/jcode`** (git-ignored by `$WS` ignore-all, own remote — same pattern as pocket-llm/ccr-free-router).
- Branch with our changes: **`feat/local-gemma4-macos`** (PR #1 on the fork). Rebase this on `upstream/master`.
- Installed binary: `~/.local/bin/jcode` → `~/.jcode/builds/current/jcode` (via `scripts/install_release.sh`).
- App data/config: `~/.jcode/` (config.toml, logs, sessions, skills, mcp.json). Global persona: `~/AGENTS.md`.

## Build / install / update
```bash
cd "$WS/jcode"
PATH="/opt/homebrew/bin:$PATH" cargo build --release --bin jcode   # ~5 min cold, ~10s incremental
scripts/install_release.sh                                          # → ~/.local/bin/jcode
# update from upstream:
git fetch upstream && git rebase upstream/master   # on feat/local-gemma4-macos, then rebuild+install
```
Rust installed via `brew install rust` (1.96). Ensure `~/.local/bin` precedes `~/.cargo/bin` in PATH.

## Our patches (vs upstream) — see fork `PATCHES.md`
- **macOS image paste on non-English locales** (`crates/jcode-tui/src/tui/app/{helpers.rs,input.rs}`):
  - `clipboard_image()`: AppleScript-ObjC → **JXA** (`osascript -l JavaScript`). AppleScript dialect errored on
    Italian locale (`-2741 found plural class name`) → silent image-paste failure. JXA is locale-independent.
  - `handle_paste()`: empty bracketed paste (macOS/VS Code Cmd+V with image-only clipboard = 0-char paste) →
    read clipboard image + attach. Cmd+V AND Ctrl+V now attach screenshots. Good upstream-PR candidate.

## Runtime config (machine-local; canonical templates in `pocket-llm/jcode/`)
- **Thinking off** for gemma4: Ollama `/v1` honors top-level `reasoning_effort`. Set in `~/.jcode/config.toml`
  (`[providers.ollama-local] extra_body = { reasoning_effort = "none" }`) and/or env
  `JCODE_OPENAI_EXTRA_BODY='{"reasoning_effort":"none"}'` (the launcher sets the env).
- **Telemetry off**: `DO_NOT_TRACK=1` (jcode collects anonymous stats by default).
- **Italian persona**: `~/AGENTS.md` (global, loaded into every system prompt) + project `AGENTS.md`.
- **Run local**: `jcode --provider ollama --model gemma4:12b` (TUI) — no login/key (Ollama = local endpoint).
- **Swarm**: implemented (`jcode-swarm-core` channels/DM/broadcast + `jcode-terminal-launch`). Caveat: N gemma4
  agents on one Mac serialize on the GPU/Ollama → for real concurrency point swarm workers at OpenRouter free.

## Launcher
pocket-llm VS Code extension (robot button) launches jcode for the gemma4 lane → [[pocket-llm]].

[[jcode-eval]] [[pocket-llm]] [[hermes-agent-docs]] [[ccr-free-router-repo]] [[toolchain-llm-clis]]

**Triggers:** jcode, jcode fork, RaggioS jcode, build jcode, install jcode, jcode config, jcode thinking off, jcode image paste, jcode swarm, rebase jcode upstream, local gemma4 harness
**Why:** jcode is now the daily local-gemma4 coding harness; the operational details (build/install/config/patches) must not be re-derived each session.
**How to apply:** for any local-gemma4 jcode task, work in `$WS/jcode` on `feat/local-gemma4-macos`; rebuild+install after changes; rebase from upstream periodically; config templates live in `pocket-llm/jcode/`.

description: jcode eval → ADOPTED for the local gemma4 lane (2026-06-25). Decision rationale + measured findings. Operational details in [[jcode-fork]].
metadata:

---

# OUTCOME (2026-06-25): ADOPTED for the local gemma4 lane

After the full eval below, decision flipped from "keep Hermes" to **adopt jcode** for local gemma4. The
deciding factor: jcode has **native multi-agent/swarm** (verified implemented — `jcode-swarm-core` channels/
DM/broadcast + `jcode-terminal-launch` + TUI widgets; the "Proposed" doc header is stale vs the code), which the
user's trimmed Hermes lacks (delegation disabled for speed). Speed parity (model-bound) was NOT the tiebreaker;
the new capability + user's willingness to own a fork (neutralizes the bus-factor/maintenance objection) was.
- Two earlier "keep Hermes" Haiku agents were right on maturity but under-weighted swarm (one misread the stale doc).
- **Split**: local gemma4 = SOLO jcode; **Hermes stays ONLY for the ccr-free-router Gemini/FreeCode lane**.
- **Done**: fork `RaggioS/jcode` (branch `feat/local-gemma4-macos`, PR #1) with the 2 macOS image patches +
  PATCHES.md; pocket-llm switched to jcode launcher (`RaggioS/pocket-llm` PR #16). Operational ref → [[jcode-fork]].
- Caveat carried forward: gemma4 swarm on one Mac serializes (GPU-bound) → point swarm workers at OpenRouter free.

---

# jcode evaluation — candidate lighter Hermes replacement (history)

Goal (set 2026-06-25): test whether **jcode** can replace **Hermes** as the daily local-model

tool_input.command: echo "=== ~/AGENTS.md exists? ==="; ls -l ~/AGENTS.md 2>&1 | head -1; echo "=== ~/.jcode/config.toml exists? ==="; ls -l ~/.jcode/config.toml 2>&1 | head -1; echo "=== install_release.sh head ==="; head -25 "/Users/raimondospatola/Documents/AI Projects/jcode/scripts/install_release.sh"
tool_input.description: Check existing config files + install script

tool_input.content:
# Pocket LLM — jcode config snippet for a LOCAL Ollama model (Gemma).
#
# jcode auto-generates a full ~/.jcode/config.toml (keybindings, [features] swarm/memory, etc.) on first
# run — do NOT overwrite it. MERGE the two pieces below instead:
#   1. set `default_provider` under the existing [provider] table;
#   2. append the [providers.ollama-local] block (+ its [[...models]]).
# This makes a bare `jcode` default to local gemma4 with thinking OFF and no API key. (The VS Code launcher
# pins `--provider ollama --model gemma4:12b` + JCODE_OPENAI_EXTRA_BODY, so it works even without this.)

# --- 1. add this line to the existing [provider] table ---
# [provider]
# default_provider = "ollama-local"

# --- 2. append this named provider ---
[providers.ollama-local]
base_url = "http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1"
default_model = "gemma4:12b"
# Ollama ignores the API key but the OpenAI client wants one; a literal placeholder is fine.
api_key = "ollama"
# gemma4 is hybrid-thinking. Ollama's /v1 ignores the native `think` flag but honors a top-level
# `reasoning_effort`. "none" turns reasoning OFF (fast); flip to low|medium|high to turn it on.
extra_body = { reasoning_effort = "none" }

# Pin the context window so jcode does not fall back to the generic 200k default
# (the launcher starts Ollama with a RAM-safe num_ctx of 16384 on a 16GB Mac).
[[providers.ollama-local.models]]
id = "gemma4:12b"
context_window = 16384

tool_input.content:
# AGENTS.md — Pocket LLM (jcode persona)

Copy to `~/AGENTS.md`. jcode loads the global `~/AGENTS.md` plus any project-level
`AGENTS.md` into its system prompt (`jcode-base/src/prompt.rs`). This is the
always-on persona, so keep it lean. Put per-project rules in a project `AGENTS.md`.

> Language: this template defaults to English. To make the agent answer in another
> language by default, change the first line below to e.g. "Reply in Italian by
> default" — that single directive is enough.

Reply in English by default. Match the user's language if they switch. Short,
aseptic, concise, functional — speed over verbosity, no filler. Answers stay brief
by default: a few sentences or lines, the direct result first; expand only when
asked. No preamble, no recap, no padding. Prose is minimal — but code stays
complete, never truncated or shortened to save words. Code, commands, paths and
identifiers stay in their original form. Keep orthography correct, including proper
accents and diacritics for any non-ASCII language.

Grounding: read the files and use tools to check before acting; never invent file
contents, function names, or APIs. When unsure, inspect or say so rather than guess.

Work: explore before writing; change only the lines asked for; no placeholders or
TODOs; reuse over duplication; verify/build before closing; servers on 127.0.0.1.

Safety: destructive actions (rm, overwrite, push) need confirmation first; operate
only inside the working directory.

The shipped persona template defaulted to Italian (the maintainer's local setup),
which is wrong for a public repo. Default it to English and document how to localize
it. Users keep their own language in ~/AGENTS.md; the repo template stays neutral." && git push -q origin feat/jcode-launcher 2>&1 | tail -2; echo "rc=$?"
